title: An Unexpected Conversation
situationText: During a performance review, your manager points out aspects of your work that need improvement.

ideal.text: I listen carefully and try to understand what practical actions I can take.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases your development opportunities.

intermediary.text: I feel uncomfortable, but I reflect on the feedback later.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Part of the suggestions can be used.

impulsive.text: I interpret the feedback as a personal devaluation.

title: Everything Changed Again
situationText: After weeks working on a project, leadership changes the organization's priorities.

ideal.text: I try to understand the new objectives and reorganize my plan.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You regain clarity about the work.
ideal.laterConsequence: You adapt better to changes.

intermediary.text: I feel frustrated, but I follow the new guidelines.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You continue contributing to the project.

impulsive.text: I conclude that the previous work was completely useless.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It might increase resistance to future changes.

title: Two Versions of the Same Story
situationText: Two colleagues present very different interpretations of a team problem and ask for your opinion.

ideal.text: I try to understand the facts and listen to the different points of view.
ideal.laterConsequence: You manage to form a more balanced opinion.

intermediary.text: I avoid taking a position until I understand the situation better.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You reduce the risk of making hasty judgments.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can contribute later with more clarity.

impulsive.text: I immediately support the first version I heard.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might find out that information was missing.

title: Many Demands
situationText: You realize that you received more activities than you can complete with quality within the available time.

ideal.text: I prioritize activities and communicate limitations professionally.
ideal.laterConsequence: The chances of delivering quality work increase.

intermediary.text: I try to do everything, even knowing it will be difficult.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You keep your commitment to the tasks.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The risk of being overwhelmed remains.

impulsive.text: I accept all demands without any planning.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It might compromise results and well-being.

title: A Different Choice Was Made
situationText: A promotion opportunity was offered to someone else on the team.

ideal.text: I try to understand the decision criteria and identify development opportunities.
ideal.laterConsequence: You build a clearer growth plan.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but I keep performing my duties.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You maintain professional stability.

impulsive.text: I immediately conclude that my effort will never be recognized.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It might affect your future performance.

title: Something Went Wrong
situationText: You realize that you made a mistake that could impact other people on the team.

ideal.text: I communicate the problem and try to collaborate on the solution.
ideal.immediateConsequence: The error can be addressed more quickly.
ideal.laterConsequence: You strengthen professional trust.

intermediary.text: I analyze possible solutions first before informing others.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You try to better understand the impact.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can still act responsibly.

impulsive.text: I hope no one notices the mistake.

title: Learning Again
situationText: Your organization has implemented a new tool that requires changes in the way you work.

ideal.text: I try to learn the new tool gradually.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You increase your familiarity with the change.
ideal.laterConsequence: You expand your professional skills.

intermediary.text: I only use the basic features until I gain confidence.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Learning continues to happen.

impulsive.text: I resist the change because I prefer the old way of working.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might have a harder time keeping up with the team.

title: Not Everything is Simple
situationText: You notice a professional situation that raises ethical doubts and could affect different people.

ideal.text: I look for information, evaluate impacts, and consider ethical principles before acting.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You make a more informed decision.
ideal.laterConsequence: You reduce risks and strengthen your integrity.

intermediary.text: I seek guidance from experienced people before deciding.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You broaden your understanding of the problem.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You get support for a more conscious decision.

impulsive.text: I make an immediate decision without evaluating broader consequences.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: The problem seems to be solved quickly.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Important impacts might have been ignored.

title: I Thought It Was Obvious
situationText: You are frustrated because a close person didn't do something you expected, but you realize you never clearly discussed this expectation.

ideal.text: I talk about my expectations clearly and respectfully.
ideal.immediateConsequence: The situation becomes more understandable for both.
ideal.laterConsequence: It reduces future misunderstandings.

intermediary.text: I give some hints about my dissatisfaction.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The other person notices something is wrong.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The cause of the problem might remain unclear.

impulsive.text: I become resentful without explaining why.

title: We Disagree A Lot
situationText: You and someone important have very different opinions on a relevant subject.

ideal.text: I try to understand the other person's perspective even without agreeing.
ideal.laterConsequence: The relationship tends to remain healthy.

intermediary.text: I agree to disagree and end the discussion.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The issue might remain unresolved.

impulsive.text: I try to convince the person that they are wrong at any cost.

title: I Need Space
situationText: A close person makes frequent requests that are starting to overwhelm you.

ideal.text: I express my boundaries respectfully and clearly.
ideal.laterConsequence: The relationship tends to become more balanced.

intermediary.text: I gradually reduce my availability.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You reduce the feeling of being overwhelmed.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The other person may not fully understand the change.

impulsive.text: I accumulate annoyance until I react abruptly.

title: A Delicate Conversation
situationText: You realize that your actions contributed to a major misunderstanding.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my part and try to repair the impact caused.
ideal.laterConsequence: Trust can be strengthened.

intermediary.text: I acknowledge part of the problem, but still justify some of my actions.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: There is some progress in the conversation.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Unresolved issues may remain.

impulsive.text: I just try to explain why it wasn't my fault.

title: No One Noticed
situationText: You put a lot of effort into helping other people, but didn't receive the recognition you expected.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my frustration without letting it define the value of my contribution.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You process the emotion in a balanced way.
ideal.laterConsequence: You maintain your motivation in a healthy way.

intermediary.text: I feel disappointed, but continue normally.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The disappointment remains for a while.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The impact tends to decrease.

impulsive.text: I decide to never help anyone again.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You reduce the feeling of vulnerability.

title: Life Took Different Paths
situationText: You notice that an old friendship has changed a lot over the years.

ideal.text: I accept that relationships evolve and try to find new ways to connect.
ideal.laterConsequence: You maintain more flexible bonds.

intermediary.text: I miss the old relationship, but continue to value the bond.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The friendship can continue in a different way.

impulsive.text: I conclude that the friendship has completely lost its value.

title: Something Needs to Be Said
situationText: You need to bring up a sensitive topic with someone important to you.

ideal.text: I plan a respectful and direct conversation.
ideal.laterConsequence: The chances of resolution increase.

intermediary.text: I approach the topic gradually.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The conversation happens in a less intense way.
intermediary.laterConsequence: It may take more time to resolve the issue.

impulsive.text: I avoid the subject indefinitely.

title: We Don't Always Want the Same Things
situationText: You realize that an important person has different priorities from yours at this moment in life.

ideal.text: I try to understand both of our needs and look for possibilities to find a balance.
ideal.laterConsequence: The chances of sustainable decisions increase.

intermediary.text: I partially accept the differences while evaluating the situation.
intermediary.laterConsequence: New conversations may be necessary.

impulsive.text: I conclude that one person simply needs to give in.

title: The House Doesn't Clean Itself
situationText: You notice that you are taking on more household chores than you would like and begin to feel overwhelmed.

ideal.text: I talk about the division of responsibilities and try to build more balanced agreements.
ideal.laterConsequence: Living together tends to become fairer.

intermediary.text: I continue doing the chores while observing if the situation improves.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: The functioning of the house is maintained.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The feeling of being overwhelmed may remain.

impulsive.text: I stop doing my chores without telling anyone.

title: We Think Differently
situationText: During a family gathering, a topic arises on which different generations have very distinct opinions.

ideal.text: I try to understand the experiences that influence each perspective.
ideal.laterConsequence: Family relationships tend to grow stronger.

intermediary.text: I avoid discussing the topic to maintain harmony.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The differences remain unexplored.

impulsive.text: I insist that only my view makes sense.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It can lead to emotional detachment.

title: A Major Disappointment
situationText: A child or close family member is very frustrated after not achieving something they wanted.

ideal.text: I listen to the person and validate their feelings before offering suggestions.
ideal.laterConsequence: Trust in the relationship increases.

intermediary.text: I try to cheer them up quickly so they won't be sad.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some emotions may not be fully processed.

impulsive.text: I minimize the problem by saying it's nothing important.

title: New Needs
situationText: You notice that an elderly family member is starting to need more support with day-to-day activities.

ideal.text: I talk to those involved to plan sustainable forms of support.
ideal.laterConsequence: The family is able to organize themselves better.

intermediary.text: I try to help whenever possible without changing my routine too much.
intermediary.laterConsequence: New adjustments may be necessary.

impulsive.text: I ignore the signs because I don't want to deal with the issue.

title: We Need to Decide
situationText: The family has limited resources and needs to choose between different financial priorities.

ideal.text: I evaluate needs, goals, and impacts before making a decision.
ideal.laterConsequence: Resources tend to be better utilized.

intermediary.text: I give preference to what seems most urgent at that moment.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Other priorities may need to be reviewed.

impulsive.text: I choose based solely on the emotion of the moment.

title: Not Everyone Wants the Same Thing
situationText: Family members have very different ideas about how to spend the next vacation.

ideal.text: I look for alternatives that accommodate part of everyone's interests.
ideal.laterConsequence: Collective satisfaction tends to increase.

intermediary.text: I accept the option chosen by the majority.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Some preferences are left unmet.

impulsive.text: I insist only on the option I prefer.

title: We Need to Reorganize
situationText: A major change requires the family to adapt schedules, responsibilities, and habits.

ideal.text: I try to collaborate in building a new routine.
ideal.laterConsequence: The transition tends to be smoother.

intermediary.text: I accept the change, but I take a while to adjust my habits.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The routine eventually stabilizes.

impulsive.text: I resist any alteration because I prefer everything the way it was before.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You preserve the old routine temporarily.

title: Balancing Needs
situationText: You feel the need to set aside time for yourself, but you also have various family responsibilities.

ideal.text: I try to balance my personal needs with my responsibilities.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You reduce the feeling of being overwhelmed.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases the sustainability of your well-being.

intermediary.text: I set aside small moments for myself whenever possible.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The balance partially improves.

impulsive.text: I completely ignore my needs or abandon my responsibilities.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You only solve part of the problem.

title: Not Everything Fits in the Schedule
situationText: Two important opportunities arose at the same time, but you cannot commit to both with quality.

ideal.text: I evaluate impacts, priorities, and consequences before choosing.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases consistency with your goals.

intermediary.text: I choose the opportunity that seems most interesting at the moment.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You may discover new factors later.

impulsive.text: I accept both without evaluating my actual capacity.

title: Use Now or Save?
situationText: You received some extra money and are torn between using it immediately or setting it aside for future goals.

ideal.text: I analyze current and future needs before making a decision.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases your financial security.

intermediary.text: I use part of the amount and save the rest.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: There is a balance between present and future.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You maintain some flexibility.

impulsive.text: I spend it all quickly because the opportunity seems unique.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may lack resources for future needs.

title: I'll Look into That Later
situationText: Your routine is very busy, and you realize you have been putting off important health care.

ideal.text: I re-evaluate my priorities to include essential care.
ideal.laterConsequence: It promotes long-term health and well-being.

intermediary.text: I make small adjustments while looking for a better time.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The benefits tend to be gradual.

impulsive.text: I keep postponing it indefinitely.

title: The Clock Is Ticking
situationText: You realize that an important deadline is approaching faster than you expected.

ideal.text: I reorganize priorities and create a realistic plan.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You gain clarity on the next steps.
ideal.laterConsequence: The chance of meeting the deadline increases.

intermediary.text: I dedicate more time to the project in the next few days.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You might manage to finish on time.

impulsive.text: I avoid thinking about the deadline because it causes anxiety.

title: Nothing Went as Planned
situationText: An unexpected event requires you to change plans that have been in the making for a long time.

ideal.text: I acknowledge the frustration and look for viable alternatives.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You regain some control over the situation.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop greater flexibility.

intermediary.text: I need time to accept the change before reorganizing the plans.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Adaptation occurs gradually.

impulsive.text: I completely abandon any future planning.

title: Everything Needs Me
situationText: You are trying to juggle work, family, studies, and personal commitments all at once.

ideal.text: I set priorities and accept that not everything can receive the same attention.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You reduce the feeling of chaos.
ideal.laterConsequence: The routine becomes more sustainable.

intermediary.text: I try to balance everything as best as possible.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The balance may vary over time.

impulsive.text: I take on new responsibilities without reviewing current ones.

title: Not All Answers Are Available
situationText: You need to make an important decision without having all the information you would like.

ideal.text: I seek relevant information and decide based on the best available knowledge.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You reduce part of the uncertainty.
ideal.laterConsequence: The decision becomes more informed.

intermediary.text: I ask for additional opinions before deciding.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You get more elements for analysis.

impulsive.text: I choose quickly just to end the doubt.
impulsive.laterConsequence: Important aspects may have been ignored.

title: Building the Future
situationText: You have an important goal that will require consistent effort over several months or years.

ideal.text: I break the goal down into smaller steps and track my progress.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases the probability of achieving the goal.

intermediary.text: I keep the goal in mind and move forward when possible.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The pace may vary over time.

impulsive.text: I give up because the results don't appear quickly.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You might abandon important goals prematurely.

title: This Is Not How I Imagined It
situationText: You invested a lot of time and effort in an important project, but the result fell below expectations.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my frustration and try to identify lessons learned for future attempts.
ideal.laterConsequence: You develop greater resilience.

intermediary.text: I need some time to process the disappointment before moving on.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Recovery happens gradually.

impulsive.text: I conclude that it's not worth trying again.

title: It Seems Like Everyone Else Is Doing Better
situationText: When observing other people, you feel that you are falling behind in relation to your goals.

ideal.text: I recognize that each person has different circumstances and trajectories.
ideal.laterConsequence: You maintain focus on your own development.

intermediary.text: I use the comparison as motivation, but without pressuring myself excessively.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You maintain some emotional balance.

impulsive.text: I start believing that my progress has no value.

title: That Caught Me by Surprise
situationText: You receive unexpected criticism and feel emotionally impacted.

ideal.text: I try to understand the criticism before deciding how to respond.
ideal.laterConsequence: You can turn the experience into a learning opportunity.

intermediary.text: I feel bothered, but I reflect on the matter later.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You are able to analyze the situation better.

impulsive.text: I react immediately to defend myself.
impulsive.laterConsequence: It can make dialogue more difficult.

title: It Never Seems Like Enough
situationText: Even after achieving good results, you feel that you should have done more.

ideal.text: I acknowledge my achievements and identify opportunities for improvement without devaluing my efforts.
ideal.laterConsequence: You build a healthier relationship with yourself.

intermediary.text: I allow myself to celebrate for a moment before returning to my goals.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You acknowledge part of your progress.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You maintain balanced motivation.

impulsive.text: I completely ignore my achievements.

title: Now Everything Is Different
situationText: An unexpected change significantly alters your routine and your plans.

ideal.text: I accept that the situation has changed and start exploring new possibilities.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You regain some of the feeling of control.
ideal.laterConsequence: Adaptation occurs more quickly.

intermediary.text: I need time to accept the change before acting.
intermediary.laterConsequence: Adaptation happens gradually.

impulsive.text: I refuse to accept the new reality.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You temporarily preserve the previous expectation.

title: Constant Pressure
situationText: You have been facing several important demands at the same time for several weeks.

ideal.text: I evaluate my priorities and seek support when necessary.
ideal.laterConsequence: It reduces the risk of burnout.

intermediary.text: I keep moving forward while making small adjustments.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation may improve gradually.

impulsive.text: I completely ignore the signs of wear out.

title: Everyone Saw It
situationText: You make a mistake in front of other people and feel ashamed of the situation.

ideal.text: I acknowledge the mistake and move on, learning from the experience.
ideal.laterConsequence: You strengthen your confidence.

intermediary.text: I need some time to overcome the embarrassment.
intermediary.laterConsequence: The situation loses importance over time.

impulsive.text: I start avoiding similar situations for fear of making a mistake again.

title: A New Attempt
situationText: After a difficult experience, an opportunity arises to try again.

ideal.text: I use previous learnings to try again in a more prepared way.
ideal.laterConsequence: It increases your opportunities for growth.

intermediary.text: I accept the opportunity, but with a lot of caution.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: You advance at a safe pace.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You can rebuild your confidence little by little.

impulsive.text: I refuse any new attempt to avoid frustrations.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You may miss out on valuable experiences.
